findings.evidence: `RootsOfUnityBaseChange.lean:70-72` composes `AlgHom.baseChangePointsMulEquiv.symm` with `RootsOfUnityGroup.pointsMulEquiv`; `DiagonalizableGroupBaseChange.lean:74-78` already provides `TauCeti.DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv : WithConv (K ⊗[k] MonoidAlgebra k G →ₐ[K] A) ≃* (G →* Aˣ)`, and `RootsOfUnity.lean:82-114` has the character-to-`rootsOfUnity` equivalence privately.
findings.fix: Define it through `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv` by exposing/reusing the character-to-roots equivalence from `RootsOfUnity.lean`, or add an explicit theorem equating this equivalence with `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv.trans ...`.
findings.issue: `RootsOfUnityGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv` is a parallel specialization of the existing `DiagonalizableGroup.baseChangePointsMulEquiv` for `G := Multiplicative (ZMod n)`, with no compatibility lemma between the two APIs.

summary: The PR adds a useful roots-of-unity target, but it redoes the existing diagonalizable base-change specialization instead of tying it to the public general API.
